title: Federal Employee Return to Work Act
short_title: Federal Employee Return to Work Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
plain_summary: The Federal Employee Return to Work Act stops federal workers from getting yearly pay raises if they work from home one or more days per week. Right now, federal employees get automatic pay increases each year and extra pay based on where they live and work. Under this new bill, employees who work from home at least one day a week would not get these pay raises anymore. Some workers like disabled employees, diplomats, and police officers would still be able to get their normal pay increases even if they work from home.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Federal Employee Return to Work Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Definitions
sections.text: In this law, covered employee means a government worker who works from home at least one day per week, or at least twenty percent of their work time if they have a different work schedule. However, covered employee does not include workers who work from home at least one day per week and who are disabled people getting help they need to do their job, people who work for the State Department in other countries, federal police officers, active military members, or other workers whose main workplace is not in a typical government office building. Employee means a government worker as defined in section two thousand one hundred five of title five of the United States Code. Telework means working from home as defined in section six thousand five hundred one of title five of the United States Code.

sections.title: Section 3  Annual Adjustments to Pay Schedules
sections.text: Government workers who are covered employees cannot receive yearly pay raises that are normally given under section five thousand three hundred three of title five of the United States Code.

sections.title: Section 4  Pay Localities
sections.text: Each covered employee will be paid using the basic pay rate for their job level from the pay area called Rest of U.S. This pay rate applies starting from the day the employee becomes a covered employee. This pay rate will not be increased under the rules in section five thousand three hundred four of title five of the United States Code.

sections.title: Section 5  Effective Date
sections.text: This law will start working on the first day of the first complete government budget year that begins after this law is passed.
